title: content.rs: format! allocation inside dedup any() check
description:
LOCATION: src/content.rs lines 90 and 118

FINDING: The dedup checks use format!("#{lower}") and format!("@{user}@{host}") inside a .any() closure that runs on every element of tags. This allocates a new String per iteration of the inner loop, for each new hashtag/mention found.

The idiomatic fix: store the candidate name before the any() call (it is already available as a local), and compare directly. E.g., for hashtags: the candidate name is format!("#{lower}") — assign it to a let before the any() and reuse it for both the check and the push.

IMPACT: O(n^2) allocations where n is the number of tags in a post. Rare in practice (posts rarely have dozens of tags), but the pattern is still non-idiomatic.
close_reason: Fixed: tag_name computed once before any() check
